reviews.author: Joseph Britto
reviews.text:
Aditi Organic Certifications Pvt. Ltd. (Aditi) is a respected certification body in India, known for its extensive experience and wide range of services in the organic sector. Based in Bengaluru, the company has been a significant player for over 15 years, helping to ensure the integrity of organic products from production to market.
Key Strengths and Good Things about Aditi Organic Certifications
1. Broad Expertise and Wide-Ranging Services
Aditi offers a comprehensive suite of certification services for a variety of organic operations. These include:
• Crop Production: Certifying farms for organic crop cultivation.
• Processing and Trading: Ensuring that products maintain their organic integrity throughout the supply chain.
• Specialized Areas: They also provide certification for niche sectors like animal husbandry, beekeeping, wild collection of products, and even Bird Friendly Coffee and Fair Trade practices.
This wide scope makes them a versatile partner for businesses of all sizes in the organic industry.
2. Strong Accreditations and Global Recognition
The company has multiple accreditations that demonstrate its credibility and adherence to international standards. It's an ISO/IEC 17065 inspection and certification body, ensuring its decisions are independent and impartial. Aditi is accredited by the National Accreditation Body (APEDA) in India and has recognition from foreign bodies like CAEQ, Canada, and the Canadian Food Inspection Agency (CFIA). Their certifications are recognized for export to over 25 countries, including the US and European Union, which is a major benefit for Indian organic exporters.
3. Professionalism and Efficiency
Clients and testimonials often highlight Aditi's professionalism and efficient processes. They are seen as a well-run organization with proper systems in place. They are committed to providing transparent and timely services, helping clients obtain the necessary certifications in a professional manner. This focus on efficiency can save businesses time and effort in the certification process.
4. Contribution to the Organic Sector
Aditi is actively involved in promoting the organic sector in India. They have certified a large number of farmers and a significant area of agricultural land across the country. They also offer a Voluntary Certification Scheme for Medicinal Plant Produce (VCSMPP), a scheme promoted by the National Medicinal Plants Board, which highlights their commitment to specific and important segments of the industry. This work contributes to the growth and sustainability of the organic movement in India.

reviews.author: Aditya Dhakane
reviews.text: I visited to take a guidance over how to work on tracenet , staff is nice & cooperative, even they take care of visitors nicely, A very nice experience working with them , As a organic certification company they will surpass everyone with their management& service .

reviews.author: Madhav Hegde
reviews.text:
ADITI Organic Certifications Pvt. Ltd., headquartered in Bengaluru, Karnataka, India, has been devoted to certification of organic products over a decade, assuring the organic integrity of the products from origin to destination. ADITI is an ISO Guide 65(EN 45011) inspection and certification body which assures independence, competence and impartiality in its decisions.
Organic Certification: is a certification process for producers of organic food and other organic agricultural products. In general, any business directly involved in food production can be certified, including seed suppliers, farmers, food processors, retailers and restaurants. Overall, organic operations must demonstrate that they are protecting natural resources, conserving biodiversity, and using only approved substances. All these practices are aimed at protecting the environment, minimizing soil degradation, and optimizing biological diversity and productivity while promoting healthy conditions.

For organic producers, certification identifies suppliers of products approved for use in certified operations. For consumers, "certified organic" serves as a product assurance. Certified organic producers go to great lengths to ensure that their products are safe and risk-free. Choosing certified organic foods guarantees that what you are eating has met all federal standards and a third-party inspector has visited the facility

posts.caption:
On this Earth Day, we reaffirm our commitment to nurturing the planet through sustainable and responsible practices. From promoting organic farming to strengthening transparent certification systems, every step we take is aimed at protecting soil, water, biodiversity, and the livelihoods that depend on them.
Let us continue working together to build a resilient, regenerative future—where agriculture and nature thrive in harmony.

Is water really the problem?

Or have we been looking in the wrong place all along?

Healthy soil — rich in organic matter — can hold up to 85–90% of water.
But when soil loses its life, water disappears with it.

This is not just about farming.
It’s about how we treat the land that sustains us.

“The Soil Remembers” is a reminder that solutions already exist — in nature, and in knowledge we are slowly forgetting.

🌱 Regenerate soil. Restore water.

Here is the link to the full story: https://nativecircle.in/stories/the-soil-remembers-episode-3-water-was-never-the-problem-our-thinking-was
Watch. Reflect. Share.

#Soil #Water #Sustainability #RegenerativeFarming #ClimateAwareness #IndiaFarming #Ecology #nativecircle

🌿 𝐄𝐦𝐩𝐥𝐨𝐲𝐞𝐞 𝐖𝐞𝐥𝐥𝐧𝐞𝐬𝐬 𝐒𝐞𝐬𝐬𝐢𝐨𝐧 | 𝐇𝐞𝐚𝐥𝐭𝐡 & 𝐇𝐚𝐩𝐩𝐢𝐧𝐞𝐬𝐬 𝐉𝐨𝐮𝐫𝐧𝐞𝐲 🌿

📅 6𝐭𝐡 𝐅𝐞𝐛𝐫𝐮𝐚𝐫𝐲 2026
At our organization, we believe that true success begins with 𝐇𝐞𝐚𝐥𝐭𝐡𝐲 𝐦𝐢𝐧𝐝𝐬 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐇𝐚𝐩𝐩𝐲 𝐡𝐞𝐚𝐫𝐭𝐬.
We recently conducted an Employee Wellness Session – “𝐇𝐞𝐚𝐥𝐭𝐡 & 𝐇𝐚𝐩𝐩𝐢𝐧𝐞𝐬𝐬 𝐉𝐨𝐮𝐫𝐧𝐞𝐲” 𝐥𝐞𝐝 𝐛𝐲 𝐃𝐫. 𝐒𝐮𝐯𝐚𝐫𝐧𝐢𝐧𝐢 𝐊𝐨𝐧𝐚𝐥𝐞, 𝐋𝐢𝐟𝐞𝐬𝐭𝐲𝐥𝐞 𝐂𝐨𝐧𝐬𝐮𝐥𝐭𝐚𝐧𝐭, where our team explored practical and mindful ways to bring balance into everyday life.

✨ 𝐊𝐞𝐲 𝐭𝐚𝐤𝐞𝐚𝐰𝐚𝐲𝐬 𝐟𝐫𝐨𝐦 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐬𝐞𝐬𝐬𝐢𝐨𝐧:
*𝘜𝘯𝘥𝘦𝘳𝘴𝘵𝘢𝘯𝘥𝘪𝘯𝘨 𝘩𝘦𝘢𝘭𝘵𝘩 𝘣𝘦𝘺𝘰𝘯𝘥 “𝘧𝘦𝘦𝘭𝘪𝘯𝘨 𝘧𝘪𝘯𝘦”
*𝘛𝘩𝘦 𝘪𝘮𝘱𝘰𝘳𝘵𝘢𝘯𝘤𝘦 𝘰𝘧 𝘥𝘢𝘪𝘭𝘺 𝘳𝘰𝘶𝘵𝘪𝘯𝘦𝘴 𝘢𝘭𝘪𝘨𝘯𝘦𝘥 𝘸𝘪𝘵𝘩 𝘵𝘩𝘦 𝘯𝘢𝘵𝘶𝘳𝘢𝘭 𝘤𝘺𝘤𝘭𝘦
*𝘔𝘪𝘯𝘥𝘧𝘶𝘭 𝘦𝘢𝘵𝘪𝘯𝘨 – 𝘸𝘩𝘢𝘵, 𝘸𝘩𝘦𝘯, 𝘩𝘰𝘸, 𝘢𝘯𝘥 𝘸𝘩𝘺 𝘸𝘦 𝘦𝘢𝘵
*𝘚𝘮𝘢𝘭𝘭, 𝘤𝘰𝘯𝘴𝘪𝘴𝘵𝘦𝘯𝘵 𝘴𝘵𝘦𝘱𝘴 𝘵𝘰𝘸𝘢𝘳𝘥𝘴 𝘱𝘩𝘺𝘴𝘪𝘤𝘢𝘭 𝘢𝘤𝘵𝘪𝘷𝘪𝘵𝘺
*𝘔𝘢𝘯𝘢𝘨𝘪𝘯𝘨 𝘸𝘰𝘳𝘬-𝘭𝘪𝘧𝘦 𝘴𝘵𝘳𝘦𝘴𝘴 𝘢𝘯𝘥 𝘭𝘪𝘨𝘩𝘵𝘦𝘯𝘪𝘯𝘨 𝘮𝘦𝘯𝘵𝘢𝘭 𝘭𝘰𝘢𝘥

𝐂𝐮𝐥𝐭𝐢𝐯𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐡𝐚𝐩𝐩𝐢𝐧𝐞𝐬𝐬 𝐛𝐲 𝐧𝐮𝐫𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐁𝐨𝐝𝐲, 𝐌𝐢𝐧𝐝 & 𝐒𝐨𝐮𝐥
The session was a gentle reminder that 𝐰𝐞𝐥𝐥𝐧𝐞𝐬𝐬 𝐢𝐬 𝐧𝐨𝐭 𝐚 𝐨𝐧𝐞-𝐝𝐚𝐲 𝐠𝐨𝐚𝐥 𝐛𝐮𝐭 𝐚 𝐥𝐢𝐟𝐞𝐥𝐨𝐧𝐠 𝐣𝐨𝐮𝐫𝐧𝐞𝐲, and that asking for help and slowing down are signs of strength, not weakness.
We’re grateful to our employees for their enthusiastic participation and to 𝐃𝐫. 𝐒𝐮𝐯𝐚𝐫𝐧𝐢𝐧𝐢 𝐊𝐨𝐧𝐚𝐥𝐞 for guiding us with such simplicity and depth.

✨ 𝐋𝐞𝐭’𝐬 𝐥𝐢𝐯𝐞, 𝐧𝐨𝐭 𝐣𝐮𝐬𝐭 𝐬𝐮𝐫𝐯𝐢𝐯𝐞.
